name: San Francisco Main Library
summary_en: Documents trace the Main Library's planning and buildings — a 1991 draft environmental impact report for the new Main Library, 1979 and 1990 commission agendas on the new building, a 1998 historic structure report on the Old Main Library, a 2000 post-occupancy evaluation, and a 2003 strategic plan — plus a facility assessment noting its 110-year history and 1998 minutes calling it the City's largest branch. The library is operated by the Public Library, whose FY2025 budget line is about $187M; a 'Workforce Dev Main Library' contract with Urban Alchemy has paid about $2.7M against an agreed $5M.
